title: SSE live-update wiring
description:
## Goal
Frontend subscribes to `/api/events` and reflects ingest + log changes in real time.

## Acceptance criteria
- `useSSE()` from FE2 connected at app root (auto-reconnect, disconnect on unmount)
- `log.created/updated/deleted` updates the Studio timeline + sidebar without manual refresh
- `ingest.processed` adds a toast and inserts the new logs into Studio
- `ingest.rejected` adds a toast and refreshes the Rejected ingestions view

## Verification
- `yarn dev`: drop a file in the watch folder while Studio is open, see the new log appear without reload

title: Rejected ingestions view
description:
## Goal
List of files that didn't parse cleanly, with reason and raw payload.

## Acceptance criteria
- Table: filename, parser, reason, timestamp
- Row click expands to show raw payload (Monaco in read-only mode) and a "delete" / "retry" action
- Retry re-runs the file through the parser without re-uploading

## Verification
- `yarn dev`: drop a malformed file, see it appear here with the rejection reason; "retry" after fixing parser succeeds

title: Watch folder configuration UI
description:
## Goal
Per-parser watch folder assignment.

## Acceptance criteria
- In Parsers view, each parser has a "Watch folder" field showing/editing the path relative to `/watch`
- Save calls `PUT /api/parsers/:id`
- A helper note explains the convention (one parser per subfolder)

## Verification
- `yarn dev`: assign the RG parser to `/watch/rg/`, drop the sample file, watch logs appear in Studio

title: Parsers view (list + manual/prompt/preview wizard)
description:
## Goal
Manage saved parsers; "New parser" wizard supports manual mapping AND LLM prompt mode, with preview.

## Acceptance criteria
- Parsers list with name, source_format, created_at
- Wizard:
  - Step 1: upload sample sidecar (XML or JSON detected by content sniffing)
  - Step 2: tabbed UI with two modes:
    - Manual: interactive tree view of the parsed payload; drag fields onto mapping targets (offset_in / offset_out / tags / source) and onto a "filters" list
    - Prompt: textarea → `POST /api/parsers/compile` → returns compiled parser, shown in editable Monaco
  - Step 3: preview using `POST /api/parsers/:id/test` (or a synthetic compile-then-test for new parsers); list of predicted Logs and rejection reasons; user can step through each
  - Step 4: name + save
- Edit existing parser opens the same wizard pre-filled

## Verification
- `yarn dev`: create-from-prompt with the RG XML sample produces a working parser; manual mode produces an equivalent compiled parser

title: Sessions view
description:
## Goal
List, create, and end sessions for the current media.

## Acceptance criteria
- List sessions for the active Media, newest first
- Create new (name + optional notes; `started_at = now`)
- "End session" sets `ended_at = now`

## Verification
- `yarn dev`: full create + end cycle persists across reload

title: Hotkey logging engine
description:
## Goal
Global keyboard handler turning hotkey presses into in-progress logs and committing them.

## Acceptance criteria
- Tag hotkey → opens an in-progress log at the current playhead, tag pre-attached
- While in progress: additional hotkeys add tags; `I`/`O` set in/out; `Enter` commits via `POST /logs`; `Esc` discards
- In-progress UI element visible (e.g., floating chip near the timeline)
- Hotkeys ignored while focus is in an input/textarea or while modals are open

## Verification
- `yarn dev`: full hotkey-to-saved-log flow works against a real backend

title: Studio view (timeline + log list + editor + tag palette)
description:
## Goal
Assemble the main Studio view from the player (FE4) plus the timeline, log list sidebar, log editor, and tag palette.

## Acceptance criteria
- Timeline below player: each log is a colored bar, color derived from primary tag or source; click jumps player to `offset_in` and opens the editor
- Right sidebar: log list sorted by `offset_in`, filterable by tag/source/session
- Log editor (modal or side panel): edit `in`, `out`, `tags`; delete via trash button or Backspace when a log is selected on the timeline
- Tag palette at the bottom: visual `hotkey → tag` reference (read-only at this layer; CRUD lives in Tags view)

## Verification
- `yarn dev` with seeded logs: all four sub-components render and interact correctly

title: Tags view (CRUD + hotkey + color + reorder)
description:
## Goal
Tags management UI matching PRD §3.2.

## Acceptance criteria
- TagGroup: create, name, color picker, delete (with confirm if non-empty)
- Tag CRUD within group: name, hotkey input, delete
- Hotkey conflict surfaced inline (red highlight on duplicates) using the backend 409 + a client-side pre-check
- Drag-drop reorder within group (Mantine's drag-drop or @dnd-kit)

## Verification
- `yarn dev`: create groups + tags, trigger a hotkey collision, reorder — all persist after reload

title: HLS player + transport + hotkey scaffold
description:
## Goal
hls.js-based player with SMPTE timecode display and the keyboard transport controls.

## Acceptance criteria
- Player loads `Media.hls_url`
- Timecode HUD: `HH:MM:SS:FF` computed from `started_at_tc + currentMs` at `media.frame_rate`
- Spacebar play/pause, J/K/L shuttle (-2x / pause / +2x), arrow keys frame step, Shift+arrow 5s skip
- Loop region toggle between in/out (placeholder for the in-progress log; fully wired in FE7)
- Transport hotkeys disabled when focus is in an input/textarea

## Verification
- `yarn dev` with a test HLS URL (env-configurable for local dev): player plays, all transport keys work
- `docker compose up --build` is clean

title: Launch dialog (media init)
description:
## Goal
On first open without a current `media_id`, show a dialog asking for the four launch inputs and POST `/media`.

## Acceptance criteria
- Dialog fields: `media_id`, `hls_url`, `started_at_tc` (with timecode mask), `frame_rate` (default 25)
- Submit calls `POST /api/media` and on success persists the active media id in localStorage and the URL (`?media=...`)
- On 409 mismatching-inputs, shows the backend reason and offers "switch to existing" (re-anchor question stays deferred — PRD §9.5)
- Re-openable from a header menu

## Verification
- `yarn dev` on a fresh DB: dialog appears, submit creates Media, `/studio` renders with the media loaded

title: API client + Zod schemas + useSSE
description:
## Goal
Typed API client + Zod schemas mirroring the backend domain, plus a `useSSE()` hook.

## Acceptance criteria
- `frontend/src/api/client.ts` wrapping `fetch` with base URL `/api`, JSON handling, normalized error type
- `frontend/src/api/schemas.ts` Zod schemas for `Media`, `Tag`, `TagGroup`, `Session`, `Log`, `IngestParser`; TS types inferred (`z.infer`)
- `frontend/src/lib/parser-schema.ts` Zod schema mirroring `shared/parser-schema.json` — single source of truth on the TS side
- `frontend/src/api/useSSE.ts` hook subscribing to `/api/events` with auto-reconnect on disconnect

## Verification
- `yarn build` clean
- In dev, fetching `Media` parses cleanly through the Zod schema with no type errors

title: Router + AppShell + sidebar nav
description:
## Goal
react-router-dom routes and Mantine AppShell with sidebar navigation for the five views.

## Acceptance criteria
- Add `react-router-dom` (only router lib allowed per CLAUDE.md §7.9)
- Routes: `/studio` (default), `/tags`, `/parsers`, `/sessions`, `/rejected`
- AppShell `navbar` with each route, active route highlighted using the ScorePlay green
- Each view is a stub component for now ("Tags view — placeholder")

## Verification
- `yarn dev`: clicking each nav item changes the route and renders the stub with the dark theme
notes: react-router-dom 7.15. App.tsx = Mantine AppShell (header + 220px navbar + Outlet). main.tsx wires createBrowserRouter with /studio as the default. Nav uses Mantine NavLink + react-router Link + active state from useLocation. Five stub views; Studio keeps the /api/health card. All 6 routes serve 200; SPA fallback handled in existing nginx.conf. Visual dark-theme + nav highlight needs human eyes.

title: Watch folder loop + rejected ingestions
description:
## Goal
Filesystem watcher that processes dropped sidecar files against a configured parser, persists results, and emits SSE events.

## Acceptance criteria
- fsnotify watcher on `/watch` with a 2s polling sweep as fallback for filesystems where events drop
- Per-subfolder parser mapping at MVP (PRD §10.3 open-question parked here): `/watch/<parser_name>/*.xml` → parser by folder name
- On success: Logs inserted, file moved to `/watch/processed/`
- On failure: `rejected_ingestions` row created (filename, parser_id, reason, raw_payload), file moved to `/watch/rejected/`
- Emits `ingest.processed` / `ingest.rejected` SSE events via the broker (B5)
- Adds a migration adding the `rejected_ingestions` table

## Verification
- Drop `sample-data/rg-point.xml` into `./watch/rg/`, see the log appear in DB and an SSE event arrive
- `docker compose up --build` is clean

title: LLM parser-compile handler
description:
## Goal
Compile a natural-language prompt + sample payload into a parser JSON via Anthropic SDK.

## Acceptance criteria
- `backend/internal/ingest/compiler.go`
- `POST /parsers/compile` body `{ prompt, sample_payload, source_format }` → `{ parser: {...} }` or `{ errors: [...] }`
- Anthropic API call via `anthropic-sdk-go`; reads `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` env
- Output validated against `parser-schema.json`; one retry with validation-error context before failing
- Compose env wires `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` (placeholder OK)
- No LLM at runtime path — strictly compile-time only

## Verification
- With a real key, the RG XML prompt produces a parser equivalent to `sample-data/parsers/rg-xml.json`
- Without a key, the handler returns 503 with a clear message

title: Parser test-run handler
description:
## Goal
Test a parser against a payload without persisting any logs — used by the UI preview pane.

## Acceptance criteria
- `POST /parsers/:id/test` body `{ payload, media_id }` returns `{ predicted_logs: [...], rejected: [{reason, raw}] }`
- Uses the interpreter (I1) with the media's frame_rate + started_at_tc

## Verification
- curl with the RG XML payload returns the expected single predicted Log (per PRD §3.4)
- `docker compose up --build` is clean

title: Parser CRUD handlers
description:
## Goal
REST handlers for `IngestParser` with schema validation.

## Acceptance criteria
- `POST/GET/PUT/DELETE /parsers`
- POST/PUT validate `mapping` against `shared/parser-schema.json` (Go-side validator); 422 with field paths on failure
- `sample_payload` stored alongside the compiled parser for later edits

## Verification
- curl round-trip with the RG parser (`sample-data/parsers/rg-xml.json`)
- `docker compose up --build` is clean

title: Parser interpreter
description:
## Goal
Pure-Go interpreter that runs a compiled parser against a parsed XML/JSON payload and produces `Log` records (or rejection reasons).

## Acceptance criteria
- `backend/internal/ingest/interpreter.go` + tests
- Implements ops: `literal`, `xpath`, `jsonpath`, `timecode_to_ms`, `tag_lookup_by_name`, `concat`, `lower`, `trim`, `regex_extract`
- `timecode_to_ms` reads `started_at_tc` + `frame_rate` from the passed-in `Media` (not from the parser)
- `tag_lookup_by_name` honors `strict` vs `lenient` mode (per-parser config; lenient creates missing tags in an "Imported" group)
- Tests cover the RG XML reference case from PRD §3.4 with exact expected ms output

## Verification
- `go test ./internal/ingest/... -run Interpreter` passes
- `docker compose up --build` is clean

title: SSE /events stream
description:
## Goal
Server-Sent Events stream for log + ingestion events.

## Acceptance criteria
- `GET /events` streams JSON events: `{ type: "log.created"|"log.updated"|"log.deleted"|"ingest.processed"|"ingest.rejected", payload: {...} }`
- In-process fan-out broker (no Redis); subscribers handled with channels and graceful disconnection
- Heartbeat comment (`: ping\n\n`) every 15s
- Log mutations from B4 publish to the broker

## Verification
- `curl -N localhost:8080/events` sees events when POSTing to `/logs` from another shell
- `docker compose up --build` is clean

title: Log handler
description:
## Goal
CRUD for Log with filterable list.

## Acceptance criteria
- `POST/GET/PATCH/DELETE /logs`
- `GET /logs?media_id=X&tag_id=Y&source=Z&session_id=W` (filters AND-combined)
- POST validates: media_id exists, offset_in ≥ 0, offset_out (if set) ≥ offset_in
- PATCH supports partial update of in/out/tags
- All times persisted as ms

## Verification
- curl round-trip including filtered list
- `docker compose up --build` is clean

title: Session handler
description:
## Goal
CRUD for Session.

## Acceptance criteria
- `POST /sessions`, `GET /sessions?media_id=...`, `GET /sessions/:id`
- `PATCH /sessions/:id { ended_at }` to mark ended
- Validation: media_id must exist

## Verification
- curl round-trip
- `docker compose up --build` is clean

title: Tag + TagGroup handlers
description:
## Goal
CRUD for TagGroup and Tag with hotkey-uniqueness enforcement.

## Acceptance criteria
- `POST/GET/PUT/DELETE /tag-groups` and `/tags`
- Tag.group_id FK validated on create/update
- Tag.hotkey unique across all tags (DB constraint + 409 on conflict surfaced to API)
- Reorder via `PATCH /tag-groups/:id/order` or `PATCH /tags/:id` with `display_order`

## Verification
- curl round-trip covering create, conflict (duplicate hotkey), update, delete
- `docker compose up --build` is clean

title: Media handler
description:
## Goal
REST handlers for Media: init-on-launch and lookup.

## Acceptance criteria
- `POST /media` body `{ media_id, hls_url, started_at_tc, frame_rate }`
  - If media exists with matching inputs → 200 with existing row
  - If exists with mismatching inputs → 409 with a clear reason (final policy for the re-anchor open question in PRD §9.5 is deferred — for now refuse)
  - If doesn't exist → 201 with new row
- `GET /media/:id` → 200 or 404
- Validation surfaces field-level errors

## Verification
- curl round-trip exercises all three POST paths and the GET
- `docker compose up --build` is clean
notes: Server struct in internal/handlers (pool + queries) registering /health, POST /media, GET /media/:id. POST: 201 create / 200 idempotent / 409 mismatch / 422 invalid. GET: 200 / 404. Added sqlc rename medium: Media to fix Latin inflector. Verified all 6 status codes via curl + compose.

title: Domain structs + validators
description:
## Goal
Go structs for all six entities with `go-playground/validator` tags.

## Acceptance criteria
- `backend/internal/domain/{media,tag,session,log,parser}.go` with structs matching PRD §2 exactly
- Validator tags: timecode format (custom validator) for `started_at_tc`, frame_rate range 1-120, non-empty IDs, etc.
- Times always stored as `int64` ms; SMPTE only at edges
- Table-driven unit tests covering happy + sad path per entity

## Verification
- `go vet ./...` and `golangci-lint run` pass
- `go test ./internal/domain/...` passes
- `docker compose up --build` is clean
notes: Six entity structs in backend/internal/domain. Custom timecode validator; Log has cross-field OffsetOut >= OffsetIn check returning ErrLogOffsetOutBeforeIn. Table-driven tests per entity. go vet + golangci-lint + go test + compose up all clean.

title: shared/parser-schema.json (JSON Schema)
description:
## Goal
Define the canonical JSON Schema for compiled parsers (PRD §3.3, §5.5). Both backend and frontend validate against this file.

## Acceptance criteria
- `shared/parser-schema.json` at repo root, valid Draft 2020-12 JSON Schema
- Covers `match`, `filters` (path/op/value), `mapping` (offset_in / offset_out / tags / source) with type-specific shapes
- Operation types enumerated: `literal`, `xpath`, `jsonpath`, `timecode_to_ms`, `tag_lookup_by_name`, `concat`, `lower`, `trim`, `regex_extract`
- Canonical RG XML parser committed at `sample-data/parsers/rg-xml.json` and validates against the schema

## Verification
- `npx ajv-cli validate -s shared/parser-schema.json -d sample-data/parsers/rg-xml.json` passes
- `docker compose up --build` is clean
notes: shared/parser-schema.json (Draft 2020-12) with discriminated mappingRule for all 9 ops, additionalProperties: false everywhere. Canonical RG parser at sample-data/parsers/rg-xml.json (verbatim from PRD §3.3). ajv-cli validates clean.

title: pgx pool + DB-aware /health
description:
## Goal
Backend opens a `pgxpool` connection from `DATABASE_URL` on startup; `/health` pings the DB and reports status.

## Acceptance criteria
- `pgxpool.Pool` initialized in `main.go`, passed to handlers via a small server struct (no globals)
- `/health` returns `{ok: true, db: "ok", ts}` when the DB is reachable, `{ok: true, db: "<err>"}` (still 200) when not — health check is informational, not for orchestrator gating
- Graceful shutdown closes the pool before exiting

## Verification
- `docker compose up`: `/health` returns `db: "ok"`
- `docker compose stop postgres`: `/health` reflects the DB error without crashing the backend
notes: pgxpool opened post-migrations from DATABASE_URL, deferred close on shutdown, threaded into healthHandler via closure. /health always returns 200 with db: ok or db: <err>. pgx pinned to v5.7.5 (v5.9+ requires Go 1.25). Verified: db up → db:ok; db stopped → db:<dial-error>, backend doesn't crash.

title: sqlc setup + generated query layer
description:
## Goal
Configure sqlc and generate the typed query layer from SQL files for the entities defined in F1.

## Acceptance criteria
- `backend/internal/db/sqlc.yaml` (v2)
- `backend/internal/db/queries/*.sql` with at minimum: `GetMedia`, `ListMedia`, `CreateMedia` (one query file per entity is fine)
- `backend/internal/db/generated/*.go` committed (sqlc output)
- A `go:generate` directive on the queries package so `go generate ./...` regenerates without diff

## Verification
- `cd backend && sqlc generate` produces no diff against committed files
- `go build ./...` passes
- `docker compose up --build` is clean
notes: sqlc v1.31.1, v2 config, pgx/v5 sql_package, emit_json_tags + emit_pointers_for_null_types. queries/media.sql ships GetMedia/ListMedia/CreateMedia; generated/ committed (db.go, models.go for all six entities, media.sql.go). //go:generate directive on internal/db/gen.go. Verified idempotent + build + compose clean.

title: Postgres schema + golang-migrate
description:
## Goal
Define the full DB schema (Media, TagGroup, Tag, Session, Log, IngestParser) and wire golang-migrate so up/down migrations run cleanly inside the backend container at startup.

## Acceptance criteria
- `backend/internal/db/migrations/0001_init.up.sql` and `0001_init.down.sql` exist
- All six tables per PRD §2 with proper FKs, UUID PKs (except Media.id which is the externally-supplied ScorePlay asset id string), and useful indexes (logs.media_id, logs.offset_in, tags.group_id)
- `CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS vector;` runs (pgvector available for Phase 2, unused at MVP)
- Backend runs migrations on startup via golang-migrate with go:embed of the SQL files
- DATABASE_URL env consumed; failure to migrate exits non-zero

## Verification
- `docker compose up --build` is clean and backend log shows the migration applied
- `docker compose exec postgres psql -U loggingstudio -d loggingstudio -c "\dt"` lists all six tables
notes: Schema covers media + tag_groups + tags + sessions + logs + ingest_parsers per PRD §2. vector + pgcrypto extensions installed. golang-migrate runs on startup via go:embed; missing DATABASE_URL or migration failure exits 1. Docker base bumped to golang:1.24-alpine (transitive deps require >=1.23); migrate pinned to v4.18.1. Verified: docker compose up clean, \dt lists all 6 tables, /health OK.

title: Pre-requisite
description: Local corepack must be ≥ 0.31 (signing-key bundle fix). Run `npm i -g corepack@latest` first. Note this in README's dev-setup section.
close_reason: garbage — created accidentally from bad --file parse

title: Why
description: npm was an unplanned fallback during the frontend skeleton work (loggingstudio-5el) because the local corepack v0.30 has stale signing keys and couldn't fetch pnpm/yarn at install time. Yarn 4 is the intended modern tooling — pinned per-project via `packageManager`, faster installs, plugin system, better workspace story for the future.
close_reason: garbage — created accidentally from bad --file parse

title: Acceptance criteria
description:
- `frontend/package.json` has `"packageManager": "yarn@4.x.y"` pinned to a specific version
- `frontend/.yarnrc.yml` exists with `nodeLinker: node-modules` (no PnP at MVP — fewer Vite/Mantine plugin compatibility surprises)
- `frontend/yarn.lock` committed; `frontend/package-lock.json` removed
- `frontend/Dockerfile` switched from `npm ci`/`npm install` to `corepack enable && yarn install --immutable`
- `frontend/.dockerignore` ignores `.yarn/cache` and `.yarn/install-state.gz` if `nodeLinker: node-modules` is used
- Root `.gitignore` adds yarn local state: `.yarn/install-state.gz`, `.pnp.*`, `.yarn/cache/` (we'll commit `.yarn/releases/` and `.yarn/plugins/` if used)
- `package.json` scripts unchanged (`yarn dev`, `yarn build` invoke them the same way)
close_reason: garbage — created accidentally from bad --file parse

title: Goal
description: Replace npm with yarn 4 (Berry, via corepack) as the frontend package manager.
close_reason: garbage — created accidentally from bad --file parse

title: Verification
description:
- `docker build -t loggingstudio-frontend ./frontend` succeeds
- `yarn dev` inside `frontend/` serves at `:5173` and renders the dark-theme health page
- `yarn build` produces a clean `dist/`
- `docker compose up --build` brings the stack up cleanly with no errors (assuming bwj is in)
close_reason: garbage — created accidentally from bad --file parse

title: Acceptance criteria
description:
- `frontend/` initialised with Vite (React + TypeScript template)
- Mantine v7 installed (`@mantine/core`, `@mantine/hooks`, `@emotion/react`)
- `frontend/src/theme.ts` exports the ScorePlay theme from CLAUDE.md §6
- `MantineProvider` wraps the app with the theme + `defaultColorScheme="dark"`
- Page calls `/api/health` (via Vite dev proxy → backend `:8080/health`), displays response
- `frontend/Dockerfile` multi-stage: build with Node, serve with nginx on port 80
- Vite proxy configured so `/api/*` → `http://backend:8080/*` in dev
close_reason: merged into loggingstudio-5el (Frontend skeleton + theme); seed split error

title: Frontend skeleton + theme
description:
## Goal
Bootstrap a Vite + React + Mantine app that calls /health.

## Acceptance criteria
- `frontend/` initialised with Vite (React + TypeScript template)
- Mantine v7 installed (`@mantine/core`, `@mantine/hooks`, `@emotion/react`)
- `frontend/src/theme.ts` exports the ScorePlay theme from CLAUDE.md §6
- `MantineProvider` wraps the app with the theme + `defaultColorScheme="dark"`
- Page calls `/api/health` (via Vite dev proxy → backend `:8080/health`), displays response
- `frontend/Dockerfile` multi-stage: build with Node, serve with nginx on port 80
- Vite proxy configured so `/api/*` → `http://backend:8080/*` in dev

## Verification
`docker build -t loggingstudio-frontend ./frontend` succeeds.
Dev mode: `pnpm dev` (or `npm run dev`) inside frontend/ serves at `:5173` and the page renders with the dark theme.
notes: Vite + React + Mantine v7 + ScorePlay dark theme. App.tsx fetches /api/health. Dev proxy via VITE_PROXY_TARGET (default localhost:8080). Multi-stage Dockerfile: node:20-alpine build → nginx:1.27-alpine serve, nginx proxies /api → backend:8080. Verified: docker build OK, npm run build OK, vite dev serves :5173 with correct index.html. Visual dark-theme render needs human eyes.

title: Verification
description:
From clean checkout: `docker compose up --build` brings all three services up.
`curl localhost:8080/health` returns 200.
Browser at `localhost:5173` shows the frontend, which displays the /health response.
`docker compose down` shuts cleanly. `pgdata/` is preserved on disk.
close_reason: merged into loggingstudio-bwj (Compose + wiring); seed split error

title: Compose + wiring
description:
## Goal
One `docker compose up --build` brings up the full stack.

## Acceptance criteria
- `docker-compose.yml` at repo root with three services:
  - `postgres`: image `pgvector/pgvector:pg16`, env `POSTGRES_USER=loggingstudio`, `POSTGRES_PASSWORD=loggingstudio`, `POSTGRES_DB=loggingstudio`, volume `./pgdata:/var/lib/postgresql/data`, port `5432` exposed
  - `backend`: built from `./backend`, env `DATABASE_URL=postgres://loggingstudio:loggingstudio@postgres:5432/loggingstudio?sslmode=disable`, depends_on postgres (healthy), volume `./watch:/watch`, port `8080`
  - `frontend`: built from `./frontend`, depends_on backend, port `5173:80`
- Postgres healthcheck so backend waits for db ready
- All services share a network named `loggingstudio`

## Verification
From clean checkout: `docker compose up --build` brings all three services up.
`curl localhost:8080/health` returns 200.
Browser at `localhost:5173` shows the frontend, which displays the /health response.
`docker compose down` shuts cleanly. `pgdata/` is preserved on disk.
notes: Three services wired: postgres (pgvector/pgvector:pg16, healthcheck, pgdata bind), backend (./backend, DATABASE_URL, ./watch bind, waits on pg healthy), frontend (./frontend, 5173:80, nginx proxies /api → backend:8080). Verified: compose up healthy, /health 200 direct and via frontend, compose down clean, pgdata persists.

title: Acceptance criteria
description:
- `docker-compose.yml` at repo root with three services:
  - `postgres`: image `pgvector/pgvector:pg16`, env `POSTGRES_USER=loggingstudio`, `POSTGRES_PASSWORD=loggingstudio`, `POSTGRES_DB=loggingstudio`, volume `./pgdata:/var/lib/postgresql/data`, port `5432` exposed
  - `backend`: built from `./backend`, env `DATABASE_URL=postgres://loggingstudio:loggingstudio@postgres:5432/loggingstudio?sslmode=disable`, depends_on postgres (healthy), volume `./watch:/watch`, port `8080`
  - `frontend`: built from `./frontend`, depends_on backend, port `5173:80`
- Postgres healthcheck so backend waits for db ready
- All services share a network named `loggingstudio`
close_reason: merged into loggingstudio-bwj (Compose + wiring); seed split error

title: Verification
description:
`docker build -t loggingstudio-frontend ./frontend` succeeds.
Dev mode: `pnpm dev` (or `npm run dev`) inside frontend/ serves at `:5173` and the page renders with the dark theme.
close_reason: merged into loggingstudio-5el (Frontend skeleton + theme); seed split error

title: Acceptance criteria
description:
- `backend/go.mod` initialised (module name: `github.com/zav1995/loggingstudio/backend`)
- Gin added as a dependency
- `backend/cmd/server/main.go` exposes `GET /health` returning `{"ok": true, "ts": "<iso8601>"}`
- `backend/Dockerfile` (multi-stage: build with `golang:1.22`, run with `alpine` or `distroless`)
- Listens on `:8080`

title: Backend skeleton
description:
## Goal
Bootstrap a minimal Go service that returns a health check.

## Acceptance criteria
- `backend/go.mod` initialised (module name: `github.com/zav1995/loggingstudio/backend`)
- Gin added as a dependency
- `backend/cmd/server/main.go` exposes `GET /health` returning `{"ok": true, "ts": "<iso8601>"}`
- `backend/Dockerfile` (multi-stage: build with `golang:1.22`, run with `alpine` or `distroless`)
- Listens on `:8080`

## Verification
`docker build -t loggingstudio-backend ./backend` succeeds.
`docker run --rm -p 8080:8080 loggingstudio-backend` starts and `curl localhost:8080/health` returns 200 with the expected JSON.
notes: Gin on :8080, /health returns {ok,ts}. Multi-stage Dockerfile (golang:1.22-alpine → alpine:3.20, non-root). Gin pinned to v1.10.0 (v1.12.0 requires Go 1.25). Verified: docker build + run + curl all green.

title: Migrate frontend tooling from npm to yarn 4
description:
## Goal
Replace npm with yarn 4 (Berry, via corepack) as the frontend package manager.

## Why
npm was an unplanned fallback during the frontend skeleton work (loggingstudio-5el) because the local corepack v0.30 has stale signing keys and couldn't fetch pnpm/yarn at install time. Yarn 4 is the intended modern tooling — pinned per-project via packageManager, faster installs, plugin system, better workspace story later.

## Acceptance criteria
- frontend/package.json has "packageManager": "yarn@4.x.y" pinned to a specific version
- frontend/.yarnrc.yml exists with nodeLinker: node-modules (no PnP at MVP — fewer Vite/Mantine plugin compatibility surprises)
- frontend/yarn.lock committed; frontend/package-lock.json removed
- frontend/Dockerfile switched from npm ci/npm install to corepack enable && yarn install --immutable
- frontend/.dockerignore ignores .yarn/cache and .yarn/install-state.gz
- Root .gitignore adds yarn local state: .yarn/install-state.gz, .pnp.*, .yarn/cache/
- package.json scripts unchanged (yarn dev, yarn build invoke them the same way)

## Pre-requisite
Local corepack must be >= 0.31 (signing-key bundle fix). Run npm i -g corepack@latest first. Note this in README dev-setup later.

## Verification
- docker build -t loggingstudio-frontend ./frontend succeeds
- yarn dev inside frontend/ serves at :5173 and renders the dark-theme health page
- yarn build produces a clean dist/
- docker compose up --build brings the stack up cleanly with no errors
notes: Yarn 4.15.0 pinned via packageManager. .yarnrc.yml sets nodeLinker: node-modules. Dockerfile uses corepack + yarn install --immutable. Root .gitignore uses **/.yarn/* (was .yarn/* — only anchored to repo root). Pre-req: bumped local corepack 0.30→0.35. All four verification points green (yarn build, yarn dev, docker build, docker compose up).
